title: Embeddings, Vector Spaces, Similarity, and Model Contracts · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for embeddings, vector spaces, similarity, and model contracts and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, safe abstention, explicit uncertainty, and named native-model, vector-store, provider, load, security, recovery, or production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for embedding-space and similarity audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Select an embedding model using its training objective, query/document prompts, languages, domains, dimensions, license, limits, and evaluated task fit.
Enforce model, revision, dimension, dtype, prompt, and preprocessing identity for every query and document vector.
Implement cosine, dot-product, and distance calculations and match normalization and ranking direction to the model and index contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Embedding Limit Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Rag Embedding Limit Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz RAG engineering team handles case 9297 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to trace embedding space, dimensions, similarity, normalization, and model-version boundaries; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test negation, numbers, identifiers, rare terms, domain drift, multilingual queries, bias, and adversarial proximity as separate embedding limitations.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Embedding Limit Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Rag Embedding Limit Evidence through the concrete embedding-space and similarity audit case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Embedding Limit Evidence, keep Rag Embedding Limit Evidence connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test negation, numbers, identifiers, rare terms, domain drift, multilingual queries, bias, and adversarial proximity as separate embedding limitations.
